hints.text: Plates almost always **full red** (the only Asian country w/ red plates); taxis = **yellow plates + yellow roofs**
**Grey car, no antenna** — the only left-driving country with a grey Google car
**Stone-block safety barriers** on winding rural roads (a great rural-Bhutan clue); guardrails are rare
Very distinct architecture: **tin roofs + painted wooden frames**
**Dzongkha** script — unique-looking letters (found only in Bhutan on Street View)
**Mountainous** (most coverage on mountain roads); **prayer flags** by the road; flat land only in the south
Road markers = **yellow top / white bottom** (national highways show the road number on top → pinpoint the road)
City signs (red or blue) show the **town name**; **black-and-white striped signposts** (also LK/IN/NP/MY/BD)
Coverage on main roads + cities; south is flat (only 4 regions covered); **Phobjikha Valley** = the biggest open flat valley
